terminal_names.description: Ordered array of terminal names for this bus

perfectly_grounded_terminals.description: Array of terminal names which are perfectly grounded

v_min.description: Minimum per-phase voltage magnitude(s) at bus, phase-to-ground, one entry per phase terminal [V]

v_max.description: Maximum per-phase voltage magnitude(s) at bus, phase-to-ground, one entry per phase terminal [V]

vn_max.description: Maximum neutral-to-ground voltage magnitude [V]; only valid when the bus has a neutral terminal

v_magnitude.description: Voltage magnitude of each terminal of the voltage source [V]

v_angle.description: Voltage angle of each terminal of the source [radians]

shunt.description: Passive shunt elements (e.g., for capacitors, grounding impedance)

capacitor.description: Fixed (nameplate-rated) shunt capacitor banks. Susceptance B = q_rated/v_nom^2; delivers Q = B*V^2.

q_rated.description: Rated reactive power [var] per phase (WYE) or per phase-pair (DELTA), length 1 for SINGLE_PHASE

v_nom.description: Rated voltage [V]: phase-to-neutral for WYE/SINGLE_PHASE, line-to-line for DELTA

items.description: Nominal terminal voltage at which p_nom/q_nom are specified [V]; phase-to-neutral for WYE, line-to-line for DELTA. Required when model is zip or exponential.

s_max.description: Optional per-phase apparent-power rating [VA]; one entry per phase conductor (length = terminal_map - 1). Stamps the apparent-power circle pg²+qg² ≤ s_max²

i_max.description: Optional per-conductor current-magnitude limit [A]. WYE: one entry per phase plus a recommended final entry for the neutral return conductor (length = phases or phases+1; a phases-only vector leaves the neutral unrated and warns, since the neutral may carry more than the phases under unbalance). SINGLE_PHASE: length 1 or 2 (phase and return are the same current — a length-1 vector is standardised to 2). DELTA: exactly one entry per conductor. Stamps crg²+cig² ≤ i_max² on each phase current and (Σ crg)²+(Σ cig)² ≤ i_max_neutral² on the neutral return when the extra entry is present

cost.description: Per-phase linear generating cost [$/kWh], one entry per phase conductor

ibr.description: Inverter-based resources (IBR): PV arrays, battery storage, or generic sources interfaced to the AC network through an inverter/converter

s_max.description: Per-phase apparent-power nameplate ratings [VA]; one entry per phase conductor (length = terminal_map - 1)

i_max.description: Optional per-conductor converter current-magnitude limit [A]. FOUR_LEG: one entry per phase plus a recommended final entry for the neutral conductor (length = phases or phases+1; a phases-only vector leaves the neutral unrated and warns, as it may carry more than the phases under unbalance compensation). SINGLE_PHASE: length 1 or 2 (phase and return are the same current — length-1 is standardised to 2). THREE_LEG: one entry per conductor (no neutral). When present, reactive capability rolls off ~linearly with voltage instead of staying flat at s_max; the neutral entry bounds Σ phase currents

p_avail.description: Available active power [W]; upper bound on dispatch for PV prime movers

p_min.description: Per-phase minimum active power [W]; one entry per phase conductor (length = terminal_map - 1). Negative indicates absorption (battery charging)

p_max.description: Per-phase maximum active power [W]; one entry per phase conductor (length = terminal_map - 1)

dc_link_coupled.description: True if the converter's phases share a single DC link, so the per-phase active powers are coupled by a net DC-side balance (sum of phase P bounded by [p_dc_min, p_dc_max]). Lets the converter circulate active power BETWEEN phases (e.g. a four-wire STATCOM balancing an unbalanced feeder) while net active power stays inside the DC bound. False (default) treats each phase independently

p_dc_min.description: Minimum net DC-side active power [W] when dc_link_coupled; sum over phases of P >= p_dc_min. 0 for a STATCOM (no active source); negative permits absorption (battery)

p_dc_max.description: Maximum net DC-side active power [W] when dc_link_coupled; sum over phases of P <= p_dc_max. 0 for a STATCOM; p_avail for curtailable PV

dc_bus.description: Optional reference to a dc_bus id. When present, the converter's DC port injects into that shared DC node (participating in its DC KCL) instead of an isolated private DC link — this is how converter stations / back-to-back SOPs / MVDC ties are formed (several IBRs sharing one dc_bus). When absent, dc_link_coupled retains its isolated-link meaning (p_dc_min/p_dc_max bound).

dc_terminal_map.description: DC terminals of the referenced dc_bus the converter's DC port spans (e.g. [pole, return], or [pole] with earth return). Required when dc_bus is set.

dc_control.description: DC-side control mode for a dc_bus-coupled converter: P (constant power, default), V (DC-voltage master holding dc_v_set), or droop (saturated V-P droop). Each connected DC island needs at least one V or droop converter, else the DC voltage is underdetermined.

dc_v_set.description: DC-voltage setpoint [V], pole-to-return, for dc_control=V (held exactly) or droop (reference voltage). Defaults to the dc_bus pole-to-return v_dc_nom when absent.

dc_p_ref.description: Droop reference active power [W] at dc_v_set (the converter's AC active power when v_dc_port = dc_v_set). Used only for dc_control=droop.

dc_droop.description: Droop coefficient k [V/W] for dc_control=droop: P = dc_p_ref + (v_dc_port - dc_v_set)/k, so the converter exports more as the DC-port voltage rises, saturated at the converter power limits. Larger k = softer droop.

dc_deadband.description: Optional half-width [V] of a dead-band around dc_v_set within which the droop holds P = dc_p_ref (no response to small DC-voltage deviations). Used only for dc_control=droop.

q_min.description: Per-phase minimum reactive power [var]; one entry per phase conductor (length = terminal_map - 1). Negative indicates absorption (default -s_max)

q_max.description: Per-phase maximum reactive power [var]; one entry per phase conductor (length = terminal_map - 1) (default s_max)

r_filter.description: Per-phase filter resistance Re(Z_filter) [Ohm]; length matches terminal_map minus 1

x_filter.description: Per-phase filter reactance Im(Z_filter) [Ohm]; length matches terminal_map minus 1

grid_forming.description: True for grid-forming (GFM) operation; false (default) for grid-following (GFL)

v_ref_internal.description: Internal voltage magnitude setpoint [V]; grid-forming only

cost.description: Per-phase linear dispatch cost [$/kWh]; length matches terminal_map minus 1

control_profile.description: Reference to a control_profile id encoding smart-IBR control laws

voltage_aggregation.description: Cross-phase aggregation of the droop monitored voltage for a multi-phase IBR: PER_PHASE (default, each phase reacts to its own magnitude) or AVERAGE (every phase reacts to the mean phase magnitude). When present it overrides the per-phase/averaged aggregation implied by a control law's voltage_reference.

time_series.description: Map from parameter name to time_series id for time-varying scaling

control_profile.description: Reusable smart-IBR control-law profiles, referenced by IBRs (linecode-style sharing). Each sub-object is optional; its presence activates the corresponding control law.

additionalProperties.description: A named control profile. Additional control-law sub-objects beyond those defined here are tolerated for forward-compatibility.

volt_var.description: Volt-VAr droop: reactive power as a piecewise-linear function of a monitored voltage magnitude

q_limits.description: [q_absorb (<=0), q_inject (>=0)] in var or fraction of s_max per q_unit

p_min_for_q.description: Active power below which reactive output is zero (OpenDSS %PminNoVars)

p_min_for_q_max.description: Active power below which reactive capability is derated (OpenDSS %PminkvarMax)

volt_watt.description: Volt-Watt droop: active power limit as a piecewise-linear function of a monitored voltage magnitude

p_limits.description: [p_low, p_high] in W or fraction of s_max per p_unit

pf.description: Power factor; positive = lagging (Q absorption), negative = leading (Q injection)

i_max.description: Array of the maximum current that can pass into each conductor at either end of a line [A]

s_max.description: Array of the maximum apparent power that can pass into each conductor at either end of a line [VA]

^R_series_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the series resistance matrix [Ohm/m]

^X_series_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the series reactance matrix [Ohm/m]

^G_from_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the shunt conductance matrix [S/m]

^G_to_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the shunt conductance matrix [S/m]

^B_from_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the shunt susceptance matrix [S/m]

^B_to_\\d+_\\d+$.description: Element of the shunt susceptance matrix [S/m]

open_switch.description: Indicator of switch state, true if switch is open (nonconducting)

i_max.description: Maximum permitted current passing through each conductor of the switch [A]

center_tap.description: Center tap transformer object, with a single-phase winding on the 'from' side and split winding on the 'to' side.

wye_delta.description: Wye-to-Delta transformer object, with series impedance defined on the wye-side

delta_wye.description: Delta-to-Wye transformer object, with series impedance defined on the wye-side

single_phase_autotransformer.description: Single-phase step voltage regulator modelled as an autotransformer (series + common winding sharing a node). The ratio is the fixed tap_ratio; regulator_type selects ANSI Type A or B.

open_delta_regulator.description: Monolithic three-phase open-delta regulator: two single-phase autotransformer windings connected line-to-line across the phase pairs implied by connection (ABBC/BCAC/CABA).

n_winding.description: General n-winding (3+) transformer as a winding-indexed list with pairwise short-circuit reactances. Winding 1 is the reference; the OPF/Ybus build a star/T-equivalent. WYE and DELTA windings are supported (a DELTA winding's v_nom is its line-to-line coil voltage; delta_roll selects the vector-group rotation).

dc_bus.description: Collection of DC buses (nodes of an MVDC/LVDC network). DC voltage is a signed magnitude relative to earth — no angle. A 1- or 2-terminal dc_bus is a monopole, a 3-terminal dc_bus is a bipole (positive pole, negative pole, metallic return).

terminal_names.description: Ordered DC terminal names. Length 1 (single pole, earth return), 2 (pole + return), or 3 (bipole: positive pole, negative pole, metallic return).

perfectly_grounded_terminals.description: DC terminal names held perfectly at earth potential (v_dc = 0). Same idiom as the AC bus.

pole.description: Optional map from terminal name to its pole role (POSITIVE, NEGATIVE, or METALLIC_RETURN), used for sign checks and line-to-line/line-to-neutral bound interpretation.

v_dc_nom.description: Per-terminal SIGNED nominal line-to-ground voltage [V] (positive pole > 0, negative pole < 0, return ~ 0). Used to derive v_dc_min/v_dc_max during augmentation when those are absent.

v_dc_min.description: Per-terminal SIGNED line-to-ground voltage lower bound [V]; one entry per terminal. Negative for a negative pole.

v_dc_max.description: Per-terminal SIGNED line-to-ground voltage upper bound [V]; one entry per terminal.

vdc_ln_min.description: Minimum line-to-neutral DC voltage magnitude [V] (pole minus return / metallic-return conductor). Requires a return/neutral terminal.

vdc_ll_min.description: Minimum line-to-line DC voltage magnitude [V] (positive pole minus negative pole). Bipole only.

vdc_ll_max.description: Maximum line-to-line DC voltage magnitude [V]. Bipole only.

dc_branch.description: Collection of DC lines/cables. Each branch is a multi-conductor (1/2/3-wire) link between two dc_bus nodes with per-conductor scalar resistance (no inter-conductor mutual coupling).

terminal_map_from.description: DC terminals of dc_bus_from this branch connects to; length = wire count (1/2/3).

terminal_map_to.description: DC terminals of dc_bus_to this branch connects to; same length as terminal_map_from.

r.description: Per-conductor resistance [Ohm]; one entry per wire.

dc_grounding.description: Collection of DC earthing/return points. r = 0 (or omitted) is perfect grounding (v_dc fixed to 0 at the terminal, with free earth-return current); r > 0 is grounding through impedance (earth current = v_dc / r). At least one grounding per connected DC island sets the signed-voltage reference.

terminal.description: Which dc_bus terminal is earthed (typically the metallic return / midpoint)

r.description: Grounding resistance [Ohm]; 0 or omitted = perfect grounding, > 0 = grounding through impedance.

dc_load.description: Constant-power DC loads connected to a dc_bus terminal pair.

terminal_map.description: DC terminals the load draws across (e.g. [pole, return]).

dc_source.description: DC-side sources (DC-coupled PV/storage feeding the DC network) connected to a dc_bus terminal pair.

terminal_map.description: DC terminals the source injects across (e.g. [pole, return]).

p.description: Active power injected [W]; fixed setpoint when p_min/p_max absent

configuration_type.description: Element configuration, as WYE, DELTA, or SINGLE_PHASE

load_model_type.description: Voltage dependence of the load: constant_power (default), constant_current (P∝|V|), constant_impedance (P∝|V|²), zip (polynomial in |V|), or exponential (power law in |V|)

inverter_topology_type.description: Inverter topology: SINGLE_PHASE (2 terminals), THREE_LEG (3 terminals, no neutral current), FOUR_LEG (4 terminals, neutral current available)

prime_mover_type.description: Energy source behind the inverter: PV (non-negative, resource-limited active power), BATTERY (bidirectional), GENERIC, or STATCOM (no active source — pure reactive shunt compensator; DSTATCOM is the distribution-system alias)

dc_pole_type.description: DC terminal pole role: POSITIVE pole (v_dc > 0), NEGATIVE pole (v_dc < 0), or METALLIC_RETURN / midpoint (v_dc ~ 0)

dc_control_type.description: Converter DC-side control mode: P (constant power), V (DC-voltage master / slack), or droop (saturated V-P droop)

voltage_reference_type.description: Voltage quantity monitored by a control law

q_ref_type.description: Reactive-power limit reference: VAR_MAX (fixed Q limits) or VAR_AVAILABLE (limits scale with sqrt(s_max^2 - P^2))

p_ref_type.description: Active-power normalisation base for Volt-Watt: P_AVAILABLE, P_MAX, or S_MAX

q_unit_type.description: Unit for reactive-power limit values: VA_FRACTION (fraction of s_max) or VAR (absolute)

p_unit_type.description: Unit for active-power limit values: VA_FRACTION (fraction of s_max) or W (absolute)

terminal_map_type.description: Mapping of terminals of an element to the corresponding terminals of its corresponding bus

bus_type.description: bus the element is connected to

transformer_winding.description: One winding of an n-winding transformer.

terminal_map.description: Mapping of this winding's terminals to the bus terminals

v_nom.description: Winding reference (nominal) coil voltage [V]: line-to-neutral for a WYE winding, line-to-line for a DELTA winding

r_winding.description: Winding series resistance [Ohm] on this winding's own coil base (n_ph*v_nom^2/s_rating)

delta_roll.description: DELTA only: coil rotation / vector-group sense. +1 connects coil k across phases k->k+1, -1 across k->k-1 (OpenDSS's standard delta). Ignored for WYE.

windings.description: Ordered list of windings; winding 1 is the reference. Length >= 2.

x_sc.description: Pairwise short-circuit reactances [Ohm], keyed "i_j" with i<j (1-based winding indices), all referred to winding 1's voltage base.

g_no_load.description: No-load (core-loss) shunt conductance [S], referenced at winding 1

b_no_load.description: No-load (magnetising) shunt susceptance [S], referenced at winding 1

r_series_from.description: Transformer series resistance on the 'from' side [Ohm]

x_series_from.description: Transformer series reactance on the 'from' side [Ohm]

r_series_to.description: Transformer series resistance on the 'to' side [Ohm]

x_series_to.description: Transformer series reactance on the 'to' side [Ohm]

g_no_load.description: No-load (core-loss) shunt conductance [S], stamped across the 'from'-side winding

b_no_load.description: No-load (magnetising) shunt susceptance [S], stamped across the 'from'-side winding

tap.description: Dimensionless tap multiplier on the from-side (HV) ratio; N_eff = (v_nom_from/v_nom_to)*tap. Default 1.0. For center_tap this taps the HV winding (both LV legs scale together).

tap_min.description: Lower bound on the continuous tap multiplier. With tap_min < tap_max the tap is a free OPF variable; otherwise it is fixed at 'tap'.

tap_max.description: Upper bound on the continuous tap multiplier (see tap_min).

r_series.description: Series resistance on the wye-connected winding [Ohm]

x_series.description: Series reactance on the wye-connected winding [Ohm]

v_nom_to.description: Nominal phase-to-phase voltage of the 'to'-side winding [V]

v_nom_from.description: Nominal phase-to-phase voltage of the 'from'-side winding [V]

g_no_load.description: No-load (core-loss) shunt conductance [S], stamped across the 'from'-side (wye) winding

b_no_load.description: No-load (magnetising) shunt susceptance [S], stamped across the 'from'-side (wye) winding

tap.description: Dimensionless tap multiplier on the from-side ratio; N_eff = (v_nom_from/v_nom_to)*tap. Default 1.0.

tap_min.description: Lower bound on the continuous tap multiplier. With tap_min < tap_max the tap is a free OPF variable; otherwise it is fixed at 'tap'.

tap_max.description: Upper bound on the continuous tap multiplier (see tap_min).

regulator_type.description: ANSI step-voltage-regulator connection: A (series winding on the regulated/output side; n_eff = 1/tap_ratio) or B (series winding on the source side, standard SVR; n_eff = tap_ratio)

open_delta_connection_type.description: Open-delta phase-pair wiring (GridLAB-D convention): which two line-to-line pairs carry a regulating winding

r_series_from.description: Series-winding resistance referred to the 'from' side [Ohm]

x_series_from.description: Series-winding reactance referred to the 'from' side [Ohm]

r_series_to.description: Series resistance referred to the 'to' side [Ohm]

x_series_to.description: Series reactance referred to the 'to' side [Ohm]

tap_ratio.description: Regulation ratio (regulated/source), e.g. in [0.9, 1.1]. Default 1.0. Start value when free.

tap_ratio_min.description: Lower bound on tap_ratio. With tap_ratio_min < tap_ratio_max the tap is a free OPF variable; otherwise fixed at 'tap_ratio'.

r_series_from.description: Per-regulator series-winding resistance referred to the 'from' side [Ohm]

x_series_from.description: Per-regulator series-winding reactance referred to the 'from' side [Ohm]

r_series_to.description: Per-regulator series resistance referred to the 'to' side [Ohm]

x_series_to.description: Per-regulator series reactance referred to the 'to' side [Ohm]

tap_ratio.description: Per-regulator regulation ratios [a1, a2] (length 2). Start values when free.

tap_ratio_min.description: Per-regulator lower bounds [a1_min, a2_min]. With tap_ratio_min < tap_ratio_max (element-wise) that regulator's tap is a free OPF variable.
